steps.title: Use the shape of the data
steps.content: The data points rise faster as $x$ increases, so an increasing curve is more appropriate than a straight line. That eliminates the linear models.

steps.title: Choose the centered curve
steps.content: A good model should run through the middle of the data, with points on both sides of the curve. The centered increasing curve has points touching it and points above and below it. The answer is D.
